message:
fix: respect --yes mode in confirm() to prevent non-TTY failure

When running `curl | bash`, the installer would fail because the
confirm() function checks `[[ -t 0 ]]` and returns non-zero when
stdin is not a TTY. With `set -e` enabled, this caused immediate
script exit.

Now confirm() checks YES_MODE first and returns 0 immediately when
--yes flag is set, bypassing TTY checks entirely.

Fixes #91

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1118,6 +1118,11 @@ confirm() {
     local prompt="${1:-Continue?}"
     local response=""
 
+    # In --yes mode, auto-accept all prompts (fixes non-TTY curl|bash failure)
+    if [[ "${YES_MODE:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
+        return 0
+    fi
+
     if [[ -t 0 ]]; then
         read -r -p "$prompt [y/N] " response < /dev/tty
     else
